meta.title: Foundation House Capital — Executive Operating System
meta.briefSource: FHC/Atlas Executive Brief for Claude (Final), July 2026

overview.objective: The objective is not to build Atlas yet — it is to become the man capable of building Atlas. Capability acquisition precedes scale.
overview.theme: Stop sharpening the ax. Start chopping the tree.
overview.priority: Ship Episode 1: script → voiceover → edit → publish.
overview.bottleneck: Episode 1 script not yet finalized — everything downstream in FSM waits on it.
overview.risk: Making Atlas more sophisticated instead of making the steward more capable.
overview.unlock: Episode 1 published + Mercury checking open + first full week of the daily deep work block.

chain.title: Source
chain.desc: God. The origin of everything stewarded. Nothing below this layer is owned — only entrusted.

chain.title: Stewardship
chain.desc: The purpose. Stewardship precedes accumulation. Money is a tool. Freedom is the outcome. Stewardship is the purpose.

chain.title: Foundation House Capital
chain.desc: The vehicle. A house built to steward capital across generations — its greatest asset will never be its portfolio, but the quality of the people entrusted to steward it.

chain.title: Atlas
chain.desc: The externalization of Antoine's decision framework. It stewards attention, capital, time, knowledge, relationships, and opportunities. It remembers so Antoine can focus on family, deep work, stewardship, and service.

chain.title: Flow State Macro
chain.desc: The public voice. Macro education that teaches stewardship in public and funds the mission.

chain.title: Assets
chain.desc: The downstream result — capital, credentials, audience, land, businesses. Outputs, never the point.

chain.title: Impact
chain.desc: Family, service, community, generational roots. The reason the whole chain exists.

philosophy.finalInstruction: If Atlas must choose between becoming more sophisticated or helping Antoine become a better steward, always choose the latter. Every feature should simplify decisions, reinforce stewardship, preserve optionality, and free attention for relationships, family, service, deep work, and long-term impact.
philosophy.designConsequence: This dashboard obeys the final instruction: no module exists here unless it simplifies a real recurring decision or preserves a lesson. Anything that becomes decoration gets deleted.

priorities.name: Flow State Macro — Ship Episode 1
priorities.why: The content engine cannot exist until one episode exists. Publishing beats planning.

items.note: Raw writing → ChatGPT script build → Antoine voice edit
Nine-stage production workflow, 01 Research → 09 Archive, running weekly

priorities.nextUnlock: Episode 1 live → content engine cadence begins

priorities.name: Foundation House Capital — Financial Infrastructure
priorities.why: Clean books from Day One are the difference between a family office and a hobby. Auditability starts at the first dollar.

items.text: Apply for Mercury business credit card
Route ALL business expenses through the business card
Pay the statement in full every month

items.note: 1 hr daily deep work block; books closed by the 5th of each month

priorities.nextUnlock: First full month of clean, closed books

priorities.name: Capability Building — Daily Deep Work
priorities.why: Foundation Phase objective: become the man capable of building Atlas. 3.5 hours a day, compounding for three years.

items.note: Script, record, edit — output-producing work only
Small Atlas-building projects; learn by building
Bond Investing, Benjamin Graham, Financial Statement Analysis

deepWork.note: 3.5 hours daily. Check each block when completed. The grid tracks the current week — export a snapshot before the Sunday review to preserve the record, or log the week's totals in the CEO Letter.

kpis.name: Business expenses routed through business card

antiGoals.rule: Do not build Atlas features that don't simplify a real decision
antiGoals.why: Atlas exists to reduce cognitive load, not to become another productivity app

antiGoals.rule: No new tools if ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, GitHub, or Drive can already do it
antiGoals.why: Tool sprawl fragments the source of truth

antiGoals.rule: No options positions without a defined timeline trigger
antiGoals.why: COP precedent — options require time certainty. Common stock only until a construction/launch date exists

antiGoals.rule: No live capital deployment before allocation rules are documented
No lifestyle inflation when debt clears
antiGoals.why: The $400–500/mo unlock routes to the war chest, not consumption

antiGoals.rule: No trading outside the four locked instruments without prior review
antiGoals.why: XAUUSD, CL, COPPER, USDJPY. Consultation before expansion

antiGoals.rule: Never mix personal and business expenses
antiGoals.why: Clean books from Day One — auditability is identity, not chore

antiGoals.rule: Never skip the Weekly Executive Review two weeks in a row
antiGoals.why: The review is the immune system of the whole operation
Stewardship is. Complexity must always justify itself against the final instruction

decisionJournalSeeds.reasoning: Misaligned with May 2029 separation and LNG bridge career goals — prestige without strategic return
decisionJournalSeeds.expected: Freed watch-qual study time redirects to CFA prep and deep work blocks

decisionJournalSeeds.decision: Retired Notion; GitHub is the single source of truth
decisionJournalSeeds.reasoning: Two systems of record means zero systems of record. Version control beats convenience
decisionJournalSeeds.expected: All artifacts committed within 48 hrs; no orphaned documents

activeReading.title: Benjamin Graham — Interpretation of Financial Statements

knowledgeVault.curriculum: The Steward's Curriculum (FHC-EDU-001 v1.0) — seven pillars: Philosophy, Economics, History, Law, Human Nature, Markets & Systems, Credentials. A governing document, not a casual reading list.
knowledgeVault.captureRule: Every book produces a notes file in /knowledge-vault/ in the repo. A book without committed notes doesn't count as read.
knowledgeVault.location: GitHub: /knowledge-vault/ · Drive: Foundation House Capital / 04 Knowledge

prompts: State of the mission — one honest paragraph
What shipped this week (artifacts, not activity)
The numbers — deep work hours, commits, KPIs moved
Hardest decision made and how it was reasoned
What I was wrong about or what surprised me
Next week's single most important outcome
A note on stewardship — was attention spent on what matters?
ceoLetter.rule: Written every Sunday after the intelligence pull. Copied to markdown and committed to /ceo-letters/ in the repo. The letter is written to the future board of Foundation House Capital — even while that board is one man.

returnMatrix.investment: Clean books, war chest build, paper trading discipline
returnMatrix.expected: A capital base with a verifiable audit trail from dollar one
returnMatrix.risk: Overreach / speculation outside the locked instruments
returnMatrix.action: Open Mercury; route everything through the business card
returnMatrix.compounding: Trust in the numbers compounds into trust from future advisors and investors

returnMatrix.investment: 3.5 hr daily deep work block, Sunday review ritual
returnMatrix.expected: Capability acquisition — the Foundation Phase objective itself
returnMatrix.risk: Scattered focus; sharpening the ax instead of chopping
returnMatrix.action: Protect the deep work block like a watch rotation
returnMatrix.compounding: Skill stacks: bookkeeping × content × code × financial literacy

returnMatrix.action: Hold the streak through homecoming; Phase 2 begins on return
returnMatrix.compounding: Discipline in the body reinforces discipline in the books

returnMatrix.investment: Daily coding, bookkeeping practice, CFA prep from Sept
returnMatrix.expected: The man capable of building Atlas
returnMatrix.action: Build real things only — FHC books, FSM episodes, Atlas modules
returnMatrix.compounding: Each real project makes the next one faster

returnMatrix.expected: Public trust — the license for everything in Phase 2+
returnMatrix.risk: Publishing delay; reputation can't compound at zero output
returnMatrix.compounding: Consistency compounds trust faster than intensity

returnMatrix.investment: Stewardship Council formation, command relationships, mentors
returnMatrix.expected: The Council — FHC's actual greatest asset
returnMatrix.risk: Building the council fast instead of slow; trust must precede responsibility
returnMatrix.action: Identify CPA candidates once Mercury is open
returnMatrix.compounding: Trust compounds before responsibility does — by design

returnMatrix.investment: Stewardship filter on every decision; Source at the top of the chain
returnMatrix.expected: Coherence — mission, money, and identity pointing the same direction
returnMatrix.action: Run every major decision through the chain before executing
returnMatrix.compounding: Alignment compounds into clarity and reduced regret

council.principle: Do not think of this as a financial team. Build a Stewardship Council. Foundation House Capital's greatest asset will never be its portfolio — it will be the quality of the people entrusted to steward it. Build this council slowly. Let trust compound before responsibility does.

wrongAboutSeeds.belief: USDJPY TTM Squeeze was pre-breakout energy about to fire
wrongAboutSeeds.reality: The squeeze had already fired — the 40-year low at 162 confirmed the move was underway, not pending
wrongAboutSeeds.lesson: Fundamentals lead, technicals confirm. The yield differential was the cause; the squeeze was the symptom. Filed as the founding argument for Atlas's macro intelligence layer.

wrongAboutSeeds.belief: EOOW qualification was worth pursuing for career capital
wrongAboutSeeds.reality: It served a career path I'm not on — misaligned with May 2029 separation and the LNG bridge plan
wrongAboutSeeds.lesson: Prestige that doesn't serve the mission is a cost, not an asset. Run qualifications through the sovereignty filter like everything else.

risks.name: Sophistication over stewardship
risks.warning: Adding a dashboard feature or system that doesn't simplify a real decision
risks.counter: The final instruction is a design law — audit every module against it quarterly

risks.name: Overbuilding and not shipping
risks.warning: Another week of architecture with no published episode
risks.counter: Episode 1 is Executive Priority 1 — nothing new gets built until it ships

risks.name: Tutorial consumption masquerading as capability
risks.warning: Deep work hours logged but no artifact produced
risks.counter: Every block must touch a real thing: real books, real script, real code

risks.name: Mixing personal and business expenses
risks.warning: A single purchase on the wrong card
risks.counter: Business card for business, period — anti-goal, no exceptions

risks.name: Financial overreach
risks.warning: Considering live capital before rules are documented

risks.name: Macro thesis overconfidence
risks.warning: Treating a thesis as confirmed before its timeline trigger
risks.counter: COP precedent — defined timeline before options exposure

risks.name: Building the Council too fast
risks.warning: Granting responsibility before trust has compounded
risks.counter: Phase discipline — Phase 1 is four seats only, built slowly

risks.name: Burnout
risks.warning: Deployment + deep work + training with no rest built in
risks.counter: Sunday is review and rest, not raw production

risks.name: Cadence collapse after Episode 1
risks.warning: EP1 ships, then three weeks of silence
risks.counter: EP2 script build queued to start the day EP1 publishes

risks.name: Emotional distractions
risks.counter: Same evidence standard for relationships as for theses

risks.name: Not committing artifacts to GitHub
risks.warning: A finished file living only on a device or in a chat
risks.counter: 48-hour commit rule — if it isn't in the repo, it doesn't exist

repo.weeklyGoal: Minimum 1 meaningful commit per week, anchored to the Sunday review
commitRules: 48-hour rule: every finished artifact is committed within 48 hours or it doesn't exist
Sunday anchor: intelligence pull → dashboard update → CEO Letter → commit, every week
Auditability: every meaningful decision produces a committed artifact — a Decision Journal entry, an allocation memo, or a CEO Letter paragraph
Drive mirrors GitHub for human-readable sharing (attorneys, CPA); GitHub is truth, Drive is presentation
DRIVE_INDEX.md is the bridge — update it whenever a canonical document moves or is added
repo.githubStructure:
FlowStateMacro/
├── atlas-dashboard/          ← this Executive OS (index.html, data.json, README)
├── governing-documents/      ← FHC Constitution, Editorial Constitution, Steward's Curriculum
├── decision-journal/         ← one markdown file per month (2026-07.md ...)
├── ceo-letters/              ← one markdown file per week (2026-W27.md ...)
├── wrong-about/              ← WRONG_ABOUT.md — the permanent error ledger
├── council/                  ← charter, advisor evaluations, meeting notes
├── opportunities/            ← pipeline notes, one file per live opportunity
├── knowledge-vault/          ← book notes — a book without notes doesn't count
├── atlas/                    ← system code, thesis tracker, weekly report archive
├── FHC/                      ← bookkeeping exports, allocation memos, IPS drafts
├── content/                  ← FSM scripts, episode assets, production files
├── Journals/                 ← Daily + Weekly subfolders (existing)
├── Archives/                 ← retired documents (existing)
├── DRIVE_INDEX.md            ← bridge to Drive
└── SCRATCH.md                ← open threads, one line each, cleared weekly
repo.driveStructure:
Google Drive/
├── FlowStateMacro YT Content/     (00–05, existing nine-stage workflow)
│   └── ...
└── Foundation House Capital/      (00–05, existing)
    ├── 00 Governing Documents     ← human-readable copies for professionals
    ├── 01 Bookkeeping             ← monthly closes, statements, receipts
    ├── 02 Council                 ← advisor-facing materials
    ├── 03 Allocation Memos
    ├── 04 Knowledge               ← reading notes mirror
    └── 05 Archive
